title: Execution Receipt (execution and reconciliation record)
description: The deterministic, auditable record of one attempted action: what was executed under which decision and mandate, how it settled, and — critically — how the execution receipt reconciles against the observed real-world outcome. Its central discipline is SDE-0 conformance requirement 6: transaction/execution success is NOT the same as outcome success, so the receipt always compares the settlement/execution result against an observed outcome and records a reconciliation verdict. It also carries the idempotency key that requirement 5 requires. Grounding for keeping settlement deterministic and distinct from probabilistic reasoning: IMF Note 2026/004 'How Agentic AI Will Reshape Payments' (2026-04-22) treats settlement as a strictly deterministic layer with legal finality, https://www.imf.org/en/publications/imf-notes/issues/2026/04/22/how-agentic-ai-will-reshape-payments-575560. Non-claims recorded here in spirit: a successful transaction is NOT a successful real action, and blockchain immutability attests to a record, NOT to the truth of external inputs or physical outcomes.

id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier for this receipt.

actionRef.description: Identifier of the concrete action instance that was executed. Ties the receipt back to the request captured in the policy-decision.

mandateRef.description: Identifier of the action-mandate (action-mandate.id) the action was authorized under.

decisionRef.description: Identifier of the policy-decision (policy-decision.id) that permitted the action. A receipt SHOULD NOT exist for an action lacking a permit decision.

idempotencyKey.description: Key ensuring the action executes at most once even under retries. Enforcement MUST treat repeated submissions with the same key as the same action.

status.description: Terminal or interim state of the execution attempt. 'rejected' means the PEP refused before execution; 'fallback' means a verified safe-mode was entered instead (runtime-assurance reversion).

settlement.description: Settlement details when the action moved value or committed a transaction. Present for economic actions; may be omitted for purely control actions.

ref.description: Settlement reference (for example a payment intent ID, transaction hash, or ledger entry ID).

rail.description: The settlement rail used (for example 'card-network', 'stablecoin', 'ach', 'internal-ledger'). Rail-neutral: no rail is privileged by this schema.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code or token/unit symbol the amount is denominated in.

observedOutcome.description: The real-world result actually observed after execution, typically sourced from one or more Reality Signals (schemas/reality-signal.schema.json). This is compared against the execution/settlement result during reconciliation.

reconciliation.description: The verdict of comparing the execution/settlement record against the observed outcome. The point of the whole receipt.

status.description: 'matched' means execution and observed outcome agree; 'mismatch' means they diverge (execution success without real success, or vice versa) and requires investigation/recovery; 'pending' means the outcome is not yet observable.

notes.description: Human-readable explanation of the reconciliation result, especially for a mismatch (for example 'charge settled but device did not reach setpoint').

audit.description: Tamper-evidence and provenance for the receipt itself.

recordedAt.description: RFC 3339 instant the receipt was recorded.

signatures.description: One or more cryptographic signatures over the receipt (for example by the executor, the PEP, and/or a settlement authority). Signatures attest to record integrity, not to outcome truth.

reconciliation.notes: Settlement succeeded for 1600 kWh but metered delivery was 1580 kWh; 20 kWh shortfall flagged for recovery.
